articles.title: PRODUCER THEORY
articles.summary: Startups keep adding data sources to give their agents better context. This has created a loop where tools are learning from each other's information. The products that stand out will be the ones that produce fresh intelligence rather than those that only combine and redistribute data.

articles.title: GAS-FIRED INTELLIGENCE, PART 2
articles.summary: AI is creating rapid growth in US baseload power demand. Only natural gas can reliably meet that need today. Renewables will play a bigger role in the future. For now, the grid must expand with gas-fired generation to keep power stable and affordable.

articles.title: AGENTIC AI IN RETAIL: HOW AUTONOMOUS SHOPPING IS REDEFINING THE CUSTOMER JOURNEY
articles.summary: Agentic AI is becoming a new shopping gatekeeper, yet customers still trust retailer-owned agents more than outside platforms. Retailers that strengthen their onsite experience and protect data and fulfillment will stay relevant as AI increasingly intermediates discovery and purchasing.

articles.title: GOVERNANCE BY PRINCIPLE, NOT BY TEMPLATE
articles.summary: Companies overuse projects and programs because they assume governance demands them, even though it does not. Modern product and platform work needs governance built around durable units, clear accountability, and adaptive risk management rather than rigid, procedural controls.

articles.title: HOW TO DEAL WITH DIFFICULT PEOPLE AT WORK: 4 SECRETS FROM EXPERTS
articles.summary: Some coworkers follow predictable patterns. Your greatest advantage is controlling your own reactions so you respond with clarity instead of impulse.

articles.title: HOW TO CREATE YOUR OWN AI PERFORMANCE COACH & “FARM-TO-TABLE SOFTWARE” (34 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Lucas Werthein built an AI health coach that unifies all his health and performance data to deliver insights no single expert could provide. Mixing different AI tools can produce apps that feel genuinely handcrafted instead of generic.

articles.title: ROBOTAXIS AND SUBURBIA
articles.summary: Technology has narrowed the gap between city and suburban living, making the suburbs far more convenient. As robotaxis grow, they could create a larger and safer transportation-and-delivery market that may ultimately outgrow or even sideline Uber.

articles.title: WHAT IS OPENAI'S PATH TO $500B IN REVENUE?
articles.summary: OpenAI needs a credible route to roughly $500B in annual revenue to support a future $1T valuation. Subscriptions alone do not suffice. Reaching that scale in five years seems improbable even with ads and commerce layered on top. This puts real pressure on disciplined focus and execution.

articles.summary: This whitepaper explains the importance of lexical data, and how grounding AI with accountable sources like dictionaries improves

WHY CRAFT AND CONSTRAINT ARE BECOMING THE NEW CURRENCY
articles.summary: A second renaissance is coming as people tire of AI noise and return to intentional craft.

articles.title: QUIET WORK THAT MOVES THE WORLD
articles.summary: Creating something new is hard, and the weight of it often sits with you alone.
